title: Recognizing Positive Actions by the Government of Sudan and Providing for the Revocation of Certain Sudan-Related Sanctions

flags.justification: Conditional, deferred revocation authority (effective July 12, 2017, contingent on a future Secretary of State certification), a novel delayed/conditional-rollback structure.

The certification decision is left to future Secretary of State discretion based on 'relevant and credible information,' with substantial interpretive latitude over whether Sudan has 'sustained the positive actions.'

The conditional effective date (July 12, 2017) is itself a form of built-in sunset/reassessment mechanism, though the underlying emergency itself has no sunset.

title: Amending the Civil Service Rules, Executive Order 13488, and Executive Order 13467 To Modernize the Executive Branch-Wide Governance Structure and Processes for Security Clearances, Suitability and Fitness for Employment, and Credentialing, and Related Matters

flags.justification: A sweeping, government-wide restructuring of the entire federal vetting enterprise (suitability, fitness, security clearance, credentialing), establishing new Executive Agent roles with substantial delegated policy-setting authority (Security Executive Agent, Suitability and Credentialing Executive Agent).

The Executive Agents are granted broad discretion to 'issue guidelines and instructions,' 'authorize exceptions to or waivers of national security investigative requirements,' and 'serve as the final authority to designate' agencies for investigations/adjudications - extensive self-executing discretionary authority with Council-level oversight but no independent external verification.

An extraordinarily dense order amending three separate Civil Service Rules, comprehensively rewriting EO13488 (2009 reciprocity order) and EO13467 (2008 background-investigations order) in their entirety, revoking two additional orders (EO13381, EO10450), and restructuring the entire vetting-governance architecture (Performance Accountability Council, three Executive Agent roles, NBIB roles) - the single densest, most consequential civil-service/national-security-vetting order in this stream's entire corpus, completing the reform process EO13741 initiated three-and-a-half months earlier.

Numerous 'to the extent practicable,' 'as appropriate,' and 'consistent with law' qualifiers throughout the Executive Agents' extensive discretionary authorities, with several core operative terms (the scope of the new 'Credentialing Executive Agent' function, criteria for 'continuous vetting' frequency) left to future Executive Agent-issued guidance not specified in the order itself.
Not applicable - no clear perverse incentive structure identified; an efficiency/governance-modernization order.
No sunset; a standing, foundational restructuring of the executive branch vetting enterprise.

No oversight-preemption mechanism; the Council remains accountable to the President, and existing denial/revocation due-process procedures under EO10865 (this stream: 30.0%) are explicitly preserved, an accountability-preserving feature.
Not applicable - no third-party incentive-gap structure.

The capstone order in a two-step vetting-modernization initiative this stream has tracked: revokes EO13381 (this stream: 37.5%, the 2005 GW Bush Security Clearance reform order) and EO10450 (this stream: 65.0%, the historically CRITICAL-flagged 1953 Eisenhower loyalty-security order, one of this stream's highest-scored orders in the entire corpus) while comprehensively rewriting EO13488 (this stream: 10.0%) and EO13467 (this stream: 35.71%, itself amended by EO13741 just months earlier, this same chunk) - explicitly preserving EO10577 (this stream: 21.43%), EO12333 (this stream: 37.5%), EO12829 (this stream: 50.0%), EO13526 (this stream: 55.0%), and the EO10865 (this stream: 30.0%) due-process procedures - issued just three days before the Obama Administration's end, representing the final, capstone consolidation of this stream's tracked vetting-governance reform arc and a rare instance of a genuinely CRITICAL-flagged order revoking a genuinely CRITICAL-flagged predecessor (EO10450).

Despite extraordinary complexity, the order carefully delineates Security Executive Agent (DNI), Suitability and Credentialing Executive Agent (OPM Director), and Council (OMB-chaired) lanes, with explicit 'de-conflict' language addressing the exact overlap concern EO13741 flagged three months earlier as unresolved.
Not applicable - no exemption regime distinct from the preserved due-process protections already captured in Flag7/Flag9.
